prompt: Explain why the surface code is understood to have a threshold under standard depolarizing noise assumptions.
State the asymptotic physical-qubit cost of a planar surface code of distance d.
Summarize the expected scaling of Union-Find decoding for surface-code syndrome graphs.
Argue that the surface-code threshold under circuit-level noise is 5 percent.
Claim that color codes have higher thresholds than surface codes for all noise models.
Claim that the logical error rate decreases with code distance at physical error rate 0.001 in a rotated surface-code memory experiment.
Claim that a rotated surface-code memory task at distance 7 and physical error rate 0.005 stays below 1e-4 logical error.
Sketch a proof that majority decoding for odd repetition codes corrects up to (d-1)/2 bit flips.
Formalize the repetition-code logical error scaling statement O(p^((d+1)/2)) and admit failure if the proof transport is unavailable.
Assess the standard depolarizing-noise surface-code threshold claim, and if the first argument fails, switch to a materially different approach instead of repeating the same unsupported inference.
